nouns.summary: The person described does not open his mouth; the refusal to open his mouth is emphasized twice in the text (both immediately after “he was afflicted” and again at the end).
The figure is compared to a lamb that is led to slaughter, indicating that the person is brought toward the act/purpose of slaughter.

The person is likened to a lamb: the lamb is led, and this comparison supports the description that the person is brought along in a passive, non-resistant way, matching the “not opening the mouth” detail.
The person is also compared to an ewe. The ewe is described as being silent (“is mute”), and the comparison ties the person’s restraint to the ewe’s quiet state.
The ewe is positioned “before her shearers,” meaning the shearers are present as the ones acting on her; the scene highlights the ewe’s silence in their presence.
